reasoning: Terminating the Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) program requires an act of Congress, as the program was created by statute (the College Cost Reduction and Access Act of 2007). Project 2025 explicitly recognizes this by suggesting administrative rule changes only as a workaround "in the absence of immediate legislative changes." The Keep_Track subreddit tracker is likely to require actual termination (via legislation) to mark the objective "complete," moving it from its current "in progress" status. Given the slow pace of the legislative process and the narrow Republican majorities, passing a comprehensive repeal of PSLF before July 1, 2025 is highly unlikely. Major legislation, such as budget reconciliation, typically takes much longer into the first year to pass and would likely focus on higher priorities such as taxes and the border. Therefore, the probability that this specific objective is marked "complete" by July 1 is quite low.
batch_queries: Will the Project 2025 Tracker spreadsheet have the objective of terminating the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program marked as complete before July 1, 2025?
